SERUAN UNTUK INTIFADA SIBER GLOBAL DI SEMUA PLATFORM MEDIA SOSIAL PADA 11-12 Januari 2024 menggunakan tagar #EndIsraelsGenocide Temen2, kita ada proyek posting massal selama dua hari (11-12 Jan) di semua portal medsos dengan naikin tagar #EndIsraelsGenocide .  Aksi yang diperlukan adalah membagikan video, gambar, pernyataan, dan bentuk bukti lainnya yang nunjukin kalau Israel telah melakukan genosida terhadap warga Palestina di twt facebook, tiktok, insta dll. Ini mudah banget karena pasti banyak berita soal pengeboman kamp, rs, ambulans, tempat tinggal, bahkan postingan darintentara Israel sendiri yang mengakui kejahatannya yang lewat ke tl temen2. Kampanye ini akan berlangsung selama dua jam dan akan dimulai bersamaan dengan sidang ICJ jaitu jam 3 sore WIB. Tolong bantu sebar dan berpartisipasi, ya. Kalau temen2 nggak sempet buat postingan sendiri, tolong bantu likes, repost, qrt, reply postingan yang dibuat temen2 lainnya untuk membuat tagar #EndIsraelGenocide menjadi trending. Terima kasih. 🤍

Hello world This message comes from my broken heart, to each of you. I want you to read this calmly, please. I am Abdullah Omar from Gaza, and I have lived in Gaza for all 23 years of my life. I turned 23 years old only a few days ago, and I still have the same feelings of pain, numbness, separation, and betrayal. I've lived this for 23 years. Thank God for everything, but these things have started to make me hate myself so much. I am still suffering from the life I live under the Israeli occupation. From the people I see and hear words that make me think about harming myself.  My life has become full of sorrows.! When I want something to come true and become sweet in my life, it seems that the opposite only ever happens. I am disappointed in the people with whom I feel safe, and I no longer feel safety anymore. That's why I wrote these words, to share with you. These may be my last words. Forgive me. Your love, Abdullah Omar 💔🙏

Due to my work as a journalist, despite the pressures to leave Gaza, I refused to do so. I will not leave and abandon the people here to save myself. I will continue my work and never leave even after the war ends. Gaza is my homeland; I've lived through all stages of my life here, and I will die on its soil.

Your love, Abdullah Omar 💔🙏

Hello world This message comes from my broken heart, to each of you. I want you to read this calmly, please. I am Abdullah Omar from Gaza, and I have lived in Gaza for all 23 years of my life. I turned 23 years old only a few days ago, and I still have the same feelings of pain, numbness, separation, and betrayal. I've lived this for 23 years. Thank God for everything, but these things have started to make me hate myself so much. I am still suffering from the life I live under the Israeli occupation. From the people I see and hear words that make me think about harming myself.  My life has become full of sorrows.! When I want something to come true and become sweet in my life, it seems that the opposite only ever happens. I am disappointed in the people with whom I feel safe, and I no longer feel safety anymore. That's why I wrote these words, to share with you. These may be my last words. Forgive me. Your love, Abdullah Omar 💔🙏

Hello world This message comes from my broken heart, to each of you. I want you to read this calmly, please. I am Abdullah Omar from Gaza, and I have lived in Gaza for all 23 years of my life. I turned 23 years old only a few days ago, and I still have the same feelings of pain, numbness, separation, and betrayal. I've lived this for 23 years. Thank God for everything, but these things have started to make me hate myself so much. I am still suffering from the life I live under the Israeli occupation. From the people I see and hear words that make me think about harming myself.  My life has become full of sorrows.! When I want something to come true and become sweet in my life, it seems that the opposite only ever happens. I am disappointed in the people with whom I feel safe, and I no longer feel safety anymore. That's why I wrote these words, to share with you. These may be my last words. Forgive me. Your love, Abdullah Omar 💔🙏

Gaza Genocide Day 287 19 July 2024 It's Friday, usually my favorite day of the week. I had a strict routine for Fridays—no going out, no visitors. But here in the shelter, every day bleeds into the next, a blend of sameness where personal rituals are a luxury. Today, I tried to reclaim a fragment of my old routine. I made a cup of tea with milk and sat down to watch the sky, hoping to carve out a moment of peace. But reality intruded quickly: ants found their way into my cup, and an airstrike hit my area of refuge.

Hello world, How are you all?  I hope you are well and in good health.  I am Abdullah Omar from Gaza, 23 years old, and I am at a loss for words because I no longer have enough words to say to you.  304 days of genocide and we are exhausted and broken mentally and physically.  304 days of genocide and we are being killed, burned, and beheaded in front of the eyes of the world, and everyone has let us down to this day.  I really do not know what more I can do.  My energy has run out, and I have come to hate being here on Twitter.  I try to tell you my feelings, but unfortunately, none of you can do anything to stop this genocide.  Please, all I want to remind you is: Go out to demonstrations and boycott products that support the Israeli occupation. Organize protests in universities. Block traffic wherever you go. The Arab peoples have let us down and left us alone. And we need you more than the Arab peoples. I am really sorry if my words are harsh and bad for you, But life here in Gaza is like hell, and we can't do anything. I hope you understand the life we live. Yours truly, Abdullah Omar

But here in the shelter, every day bleeds into the next, a blend of sameness where personal rituals are a luxury. Today, I tried to reclaim a fragment of my old routine. I made a cup of tea with milk and sat down to watch the sky, hoping to carve out a moment of peace. But reality intruded quickly: ants found their way into my cup, and an airstrike hit my area of refuge.

Hello world, How are you all?  I hope you are well and in good health.  I am Abdullah Omar from Gaza, 23 years old, and I am at a loss for words because I no longer have enough words to say to you.  304 days of genocide and we are exhausted and broken mentally and physically.  304 days of genocide and we are being killed, burned, and beheaded in front of the eyes of the world, and everyone has let us down to this day.  I really do not know what more I can do.  My energy has run out, and I have come to hate being here on Twitter.  I try to tell you my feelings, but unfortunately, none of you can do anything to stop this genocide.  Please, all I want to remind you is: Go out to demonstrations and boycott products that support the Israeli occupation. Organize protests in universities. Block traffic wherever you go. The Arab peoples have let us down and left us alone. And we need you more than the Arab peoples. I am really sorry if my words are harsh and bad for you, But life here in Gaza is like hell, and we can't do anything. I hope you understand the life we live. Yours truly, Abdullah Omar

I am Abdullah Omar from Gaza, 23 years old, and I am at a loss for words because I no longer have enough words to say to you.  304 days of genocide and we are exhausted and broken mentally and physically.  304 days of genocide and we are being killed, burned, and beheaded in front of the eyes of the world, and everyone has let us down to this day. I try to tell you my feelings, but unfortunately, none of you can do anything to stop this genocide.  Please, all I want to remind you is: Go out to demonstrations and boycott products that support the Israeli occupation. Organize protests in universities. Block traffic wherever you go. The Arab peoples have let us down and left us alone. And we need you more than the Arab peoples. “You claim my land belongs to you, yet you destroy every part of it. I would die for my land, can you say the same?” I love Gaza so much! In every corner, I see parts of myself: a dream I’ve had, a moment with a friend or family. I’ve walked every street, shopped in every shop. I’ve always wished to have kids with my wife, so I can show them around and tell them stories about every spot in Gaza, create new memories with them, and teach them how to love their land. A couple of months ago, I got injured by the Israeli occupation; so I had to leave my beloved Gaza to get treatment. However, I couldn’t stay there anymore! It didn’t smell the same, it didn’t feel the same!  I wanted to be around Gazans, around my family! I knew it was a bad decision to come back, my family would call me and say: “Sameh, don’t come back! It’s safer for you in Egypt”, but they don’t understand! Because yes, I would risk my life to be in Gaza, to die in Gaza. “On July 20th, Sameh Al-Batran was killed by the Israeli occupation, along with many of his family members, as they were seeking safety in their own house”

“I tried my best to stay strong and make people feel better, even when everything around me was falling apart” I don't understand many things, like why all of this had to happen to us. I take pride in being reliable and positive, in never knowing despair. Even when things were tough, I aimed to shine like a lamp, to work hard and keep moving forward for my loved ones. But on July 26, 2024,  I was forced to step back.  I left this world succumbing to my wounds, and it pains me deeply to think about the suffering my family endured. As I faced my own end, I carried with me the weight of their tears and the memory of our struggle. But we are together now and that is all that matters.  I know that my hard work in life was not in vain, as I did it for more than just money. I wanted to leave behind a reminder that no matter how dark the world may seem, there’s always room for a bit of light.  Don’t forget that we are all the same. Just because we endured more suffering doesn’t mean we are stronger and therefore meant to live like this. Please keep speaking up, remember me and all of the martyrs, don't give up on a free Palestine.

“You claim my land belongs to you, yet you destroy every part of it. I would die for my land, can you say the same?” I love Gaza so much! In every corner, I see parts of myself: a dream I’ve had, a moment with a friend or family. I’ve walked every street, shopped in every shop. I’ve always wished to have kids with my wife, so I can show them around and tell them stories about every spot in Gaza, create new memories with them, and teach them how to love their land. A couple of months ago, I got injured by the Israeli occupation; so I had to leave my beloved Gaza to get treatment. However, I couldn’t stay there anymore! It didn’t smell the same, it didn’t feel the same!  I wanted to be around Gazans, around my family! I knew it was a bad decision to come back, my family would call me and say: “Sameh, don’t come back! It’s safer for you in Egypt”, but they don’t understand! Because yes, I would risk my life to be in Gaza, to die in Gaza. “On July 20th, Sameh Al-Batran was killed by the Israeli occupation, along with many of his family members, as they were seeking safety in their own house”

“I tried my best to stay strong and make people feel better, even when everything around me was falling apart” I don't understand many things, like why all of this had to happen to us. I take pride in being reliable and positive, in never knowing despair. Even when things were tough, I aimed to shine like a lamp, to work hard and keep moving forward for my loved ones. But on July 26, 2024,  I was forced to step back.  I left this world succumbing to my wounds, and it pains me deeply to think about the suffering my family endured. As I faced my own end, I carried with me the weight of their tears and the memory of our struggle. But we are together now and that is all that matters.  I know that my hard work in life was not in vain, as I did it for more than just money. I wanted to leave behind a reminder that no matter how dark the world may seem, there’s always room for a bit of light.  Don’t forget that we are all the same. Just because we endured more suffering doesn’t mean we are stronger and therefore meant to live like this. Please keep speaking up, remember me and all of the martyrs, don't give up on a free Palestine.

In every corner, I see parts of myself: a dream I’ve had, a moment with a friend or family. I’ve walked every street, shopped in every shop. I’ve always wished to have kids with my wife, so I can show them around and tell them stories about every spot in Gaza, create new memories with them, and teach them how to love their land. A couple of months ago, I got injured by the Israeli occupation; so I had to leave my beloved Gaza to get treatment. However, I couldn’t stay there anymore! It didn’t smell the same, it didn’t feel the same!  I wanted to be around Gazans, around my family! I knew it was a bad decision to come back, my family would call me and say: “Sameh, don’t come back! It’s safer for you in Egypt”, but they don’t understand! Because yes, I would risk my life to be in Gaza, to die in Gaza. “On July 20th, Sameh Al-Batran was killed by the Israeli occupation, along with many of his family members, as they were seeking safety in their own house”

“I tried my best to stay strong and make people feel better, even when everything around me was falling apart” I don't understand many things, like why all of this had to happen to us. I take pride in being reliable and positive, in never knowing despair. Even when things were tough, I aimed to shine like a lamp, to work hard and keep moving forward for my loved ones. But on July 26, 2024,  I was forced to step back.  I left this world succumbing to my wounds, and it pains me deeply to think about the suffering my family endured. As I faced my own end, I carried with me the weight of their tears and the memory of our struggle. But we are together now and that is all that matters.  I know that my hard work in life was not in vain, as I did it for more than just money. I wanted to leave behind a reminder that no matter how dark the world may seem, there’s always room for a bit of light.  Don’t forget that we are all the same. Just because we endured more suffering doesn’t mean we are stronger and therefore meant to live like this. Please keep speaking up, remember me and all of the martyrs, don't give up on a free Palestine.

Hello world, How are you all?  I hope you are well and in good health.  I am Abdullah Omar from Gaza, 23 years old, and I am at a loss for words because I no longer have enough words to say to you.  304 days of genocide and we are exhausted and broken mentally and physically.  304 days of genocide and we are being killed, burned, and beheaded in front of the eyes of the world, and everyone has let us down to this day.  I really do not know what more I can do.  My energy has run out, and I have come to hate being here on Twitter.  I try to tell you my feelings, but unfortunately, none of you can do anything to stop this genocide.  Please, all I want to remind you is: Go out to demonstrations and boycott products that support the Israeli occupation. Organize protests in universities. Block traffic wherever you go. The Arab peoples have let us down and left us alone. And we need you more than the Arab peoples. I am really sorry if my words are harsh and bad for you, But life here in Gaza is like hell, and we can't do anything. I hope you understand the life we live. Yours truly, Abdullah Omar

“You claim my land belongs to you, yet you destroy every part of it. I would die for my land, can you say the same?” I love Gaza so much! In every corner, I see parts of myself: a dream I’ve had, a moment with a friend or family. I’ve walked every street, shopped in every shop. I’ve always wished to have kids with my wife, so I can show them around and tell them stories about every spot in Gaza, create new memories with them, and teach them how to love their land. A couple of months ago, I got injured by the Israeli occupation; so I had to leave my beloved Gaza to get treatment. However, I couldn’t stay there anymore! It didn’t smell the same, it didn’t feel the same!  I wanted to be around Gazans, around my family! I knew it was a bad decision to come back, my family would call me and say: “Sameh, don’t come back! It’s safer for you in Egypt”, but they don’t understand! Because yes, I would risk my life to be in Gaza, to die in Gaza. “On July 20th, Sameh Al-Batran was killed by the Israeli occupation, along with many of his family members, as they were seeking safety in their own house”

“I tried my best to stay strong and make people feel better, even when everything around me was falling apart” I don't understand many things, like why all of this had to happen to us. I take pride in being reliable and positive, in never knowing despair. Even when things were tough, I aimed to shine like a lamp, to work hard and keep moving forward for my loved ones. But on July 26, 2024,  I was forced to step back.  I left this world succumbing to my wounds, and it pains me deeply to think about the suffering my family endured. As I faced my own end, I carried with me the weight of their tears and the memory of our struggle. But we are together now and that is all that matters.  I know that my hard work in life was not in vain, as I did it for more than just money. I wanted to leave behind a reminder that no matter how dark the world may seem, there’s always room for a bit of light.  Don’t forget that we are all the same. Just because we endured more suffering doesn’t mean we are stronger and therefore meant to live like this. Please keep speaking up, remember me and all of the martyrs, don't give up on a free Palestine.
